file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16-AMENDMENT TO THE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT BETWEEN INGRAM MICRO AND NETGEAR.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be voidable.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be assigned by either party with prior written notification to the other party. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement with such notification will be fully valid.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the agreement *can* be assigned with simple notification, while other sections may imply the need for approval, creating uncertainty about the assignment process. This pertubation has a contradiction in Section 18H
perturbation.contradicted_text: H. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be voidable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement can be assigned with simple notification, while the contradicted text requires prior written permission. This creates a direct conflict regarding the assignment process, as one allows assignment with notification, while the other requires permission. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor agrees that the foregoing Discount is only applicable to Products included on the NETGEAR Price List on the Effective Date of this Agreement. NETGEAR reserves the right to add Products to the Price List at its sole discretion and any such additional Products shall be offered to Distributor at discounts to be determined at that time.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor agrees that the foregoing Discount is applicable to all Products included on the NETGEAR Price List, regardless of the Effective Date of this Agreement. NETGEAR will add Products to the Price List when there is a mutual agreement with distributor and any such additional Products shall be offered to Distributor at the same initial discount.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the discount and pricing of new products added to the Price List. The original text grants NETGEAR sole discretion to add products and determine discounts while the changed text mandates mutual agreement and the same initial discount, making NETGEAR's authority unclear in other related sections. This pertubation has a contradiction in EXHIBIT 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT 1 DISCOUNT SCHEDULE The initial Discount offered Distributor for purchase or license of NETGEAR Products included on the NETGEAR Price List in effect on the Effective Date of this Agreement is [*] off of the then current NETGEAR list price. Distributor agrees that the foregoing Discount is only applicable to Products included on the NETGEAR Price List on the Effective Date of this Agreement. NETGEAR reserves the right to add Products to the Price List at its sole discretion and any such additional Products shall be offered to Distributor at discounts to be determined at that time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction regarding the terms for adding new products to the Price List and the discounts applied to them. The original text states NETGEAR has 'sole discretion' to add products and determine their discounts. The changed text requires 'mutual agreement' and the same initial discount for new products. This directly contradicts the section in EXHIBIT 1, which reiterates NETGEAR's sole discretion. This shift from unilateral to mutual agreement creates a clear and impactful conflict concerning NETGEAR's rights and obligations, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms of this Amendment, the Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended to be the United States and [*].
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms of this Amendment, the Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended to be the United States, [*] and Canada.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. Section 2 in Amendment #2 states 'TERRITORY, is amended to be United States, [*]'. However, Amendment #1 says that, the territory now includes Canada. There is now in-text contradiction between 2 different amendments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AMENDMENT #2 TO THE DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT THIS AMENDMENT (the "Amendment") is entered into this 15th day of July 1998, by and between INGRAM MICRO INC. ("Ingram") and NETGEAR, INC. ("Vendor"). The parties have agreed to amend their Distribution Agreement ("Agreement") dated October 1,1996. 1. Section 2, TERRITORY, is amended to be United States, [*].
perturbation.justification: The original agreement in Amendment #2 defines the territory as the 'United States, [*]'. The changed text in Amendment #1 expands this territory to include 'United States, [*] and Canada'. This directly contradicts the territory defined in Amendment #2, creating an in-text contradiction. The second amendment limits the territory, while the first expands the territory, a irreconcilable conflict. This falls under 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the term "Territory" is defined inconsistently in different parts of the contract.
